what: 4. Build heatmap matrices from EasyProtein objects
title: ComplexHeatmap Visualization with EasyProtein
text: EasyProtein stores several assays SummarizedExperiment object. heatmaps, two views especially common: sample-level matrix: one column per biological replicate condition-level matrix: one column per condition, usually mean median across replicates visual stability, examples select top variable proteins instead plotting every detected protein vignette. input data contain duplicated gene symbols, keep row names unique protein IDs computation. can still display gene symbols later adding row annotations selecting representative rows aggregation.
code: z_df <- se2scale(se_focus, rescale = TRUE)  z_mtx <- as.matrix(z_df[, colnames(se_focus), drop = FALSE]) rownames(z_mtx) <- rownames(se_focus) z_mtx[!is.finite(z_mtx)] <- 0  top_n <- min(300, nrow(z_mtx)) row_var <- matrixStats::rowVars(z_mtx) names(row_var) <- rownames(z_mtx) top_variable_genes <- names(sort(row_var, decreasing = TRUE))[seq_len(top_n)]  z_top <- z_mtx[top_variable_genes, , drop = FALSE]  condition_mean_mtx <- calc_gene_mean_by_condition(   se = se_focus,   assay_name = "conc",   method = "mean",   condition_col = "age" )  condition_mean_z <- scale_mtx(condition_mean_mtx) condition_mean_z[!is.finite(condition_mean_z)] <- 0 condition_mean_top <- condition_mean_z[top_variable_genes, , drop = FALSE]  dim(z_top) #> [1] 300  15 dim(condition_mean_top) #> [1] 300   3

title: ComplexHeatmap Visualization with EasyProtein
text: first heatmap keeps biological replicates separate columns. view useful checking whether replicates behave consistently inside age group.
code: ht_sample <- ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(   z_top,   name = "Z-score",   cluster_rows = TRUE,   cluster_columns = FALSE,   show_row_names = FALSE,   show_column_names = FALSE,   column_split = se_focus$age,   column_title = NULL,   use_raster = TRUE )  ht_sample

title: ComplexHeatmap Visualization with EasyProtein
text: question overall pattern across conditions, average replicates first. makes heatmap smaller easier interpret.  Use sample-level heatmap inspect replicate behavior. Use condition-level heatmap communicate main biological trend.
code: ht_condition <- ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(   condition_mean_top,   name = "Z-score",   cluster_rows = TRUE,   cluster_columns = FALSE,   show_row_names = FALSE,   show_column_names = TRUE,   column_title = NULL,   use_raster = TRUE )  ht_condition

text: Column annotations make experimental design explicit. example colors age groups replicate numbers sample-level heatmap.
code: age_col <- c(   "1w" = "#1F77B4",   "4w" = "#2CA02C",   "8w" = "#D62728" )  rep_levels <- levels(se_focus$rep) rep_col <- setNames(   grDevices::hcl.colors(length(rep_levels), palette = "Dark 3"),   rep_levels )  top_anno <- ComplexHeatmap::HeatmapAnnotation(   age = se_focus$age,   rep = se_focus$rep,   col = list(     age = age_col,     rep = rep_col   ),   show_annotation_name = TRUE )  ht_anno <- ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(   z_top,   name = "Z-score",   cluster_rows = TRUE,   cluster_columns = FALSE,   show_row_names = FALSE,   show_column_names = FALSE,   top_annotation = top_anno,   column_split = se_focus$age,   column_title = NULL,   use_raster = TRUE )  ht_anno

title: ComplexHeatmap Visualization with EasyProtein
text: example identifies proteins contribute strongly PC1 draws focused heatmap. Positive negative contributors used row splits.
code: conc_mtx <- SummarizedExperiment::assay(se_focus, "conc") pca_res <- FactoMineR::PCA(t(conc_mtx), scale.unit = TRUE, graph = FALSE)  pc1_genes <- dplyr::bind_rows(   get_pc_contributors(pca_res, se_focus, pc = 1, n = 30)$positive,   get_pc_contributors(pca_res, se_focus, pc = 1, n = 30)$negative ) %>%   dplyr::filter(gene %in% rownames(z_mtx)) %>%   dplyr::distinct(gene, .keep_all = TRUE)  pc1_label_genes <- pc1_genes %>%   dplyr::group_by(type) %>%   dplyr::slice_head(n = 5) %>%   dplyr::ungroup() %>%   dplyr::pull(gene)  head(pc1_genes) #>     gene     value     type #> 1   Rpl7 0.9931828 Positive #> 2   Xpo5 0.9922783 Positive #> 3  Rab7a 0.9910809 Positive #> 4  Psmd3 0.9906672 Positive #> 5  Ap1g1 0.9904143 Positive #> 6 Polr2j 0.9900570 Positive pc1_mtx <- z_mtx[pc1_genes$gene, , drop = FALSE] pc1_row_labels <- ifelse(rownames(pc1_mtx) %in% pc1_label_genes, rownames(pc1_mtx), "")  ht_pc1 <- ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(   pc1_mtx,   name = "Z-score",   cluster_rows = TRUE,   cluster_columns = FALSE,   row_split = pc1_genes$type,   show_row_names = TRUE,   row_labels = pc1_row_labels,   show_column_names = FALSE,   top_annotation = top_anno,   column_split = se_focus$age,   column_title = NULL,   use_raster = TRUE )  ht_pc1

what: 9.1 Enrichment analysis for each k-means cluster
title: ComplexHeatmap Visualization with EasyProtein
text: heatmap clusters defined, cluster can treated gene set functional interpretation. code runs GO enrichment every km_cluster prints top terms per cluster clusterProfiler mouse annotation database available.
code: cluster_gene_list <- split(row_cluster_df$gene, row_cluster_df$km_cluster)  if (   requireNamespace("clusterProfiler", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("org.Mm.eg.db", quietly = TRUE) ) {   km_go_list <- purrr::imap(cluster_gene_list, function(genes, cluster_name) {     genes <- unique(stats::na.omit(genes))      if (length(genes) < 5) {       return(NULL)     }      res <- tryCatch(       enrichment_analysis(         gene = genes,         db = "GO",         species = "mouse",         keyType = "SYMBOL",         qvalue_cutoff = 0.05       ),       error = function(e) {         message(cluster_name, ": enrichment skipped - ", conditionMessage(e))         NULL       }     )      if (is.null(res) || nrow(res) == 0) {       return(NULL)     }      dplyr::mutate(as.data.frame(res), km_cluster = cluster_name, .before = 1)   })    km_go_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(km_go_list)    if (nrow(km_go_df) > 0) {     km_go_df %>%       dplyr::group_by(km_cluster) %>%       dplyr::slice_head(n = 5) %>%       dplyr::ungroup()   } else {     message("No enriched GO terms were found for the current k-means clusters.")   } } else {   message("Install 'clusterProfiler' and 'org.Mm.eg.db' to run per-cluster GO enrichment.") } #>  #>  #> # title: ComplexHeatmap Visualization with EasyProtein

code: scale_df <- se2scale(se, rescale = TRUE) %>%   dplyr::mutate(gene_symbol = stringr::str_extract(Genes, "^[^;]+")) %>%   dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene_symbol), gene_symbol != "") %>%   dplyr::group_by(gene_symbol) %>%   dplyr::summarise(     dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(colnames(se)), mean, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  gene_z_mtx <- as.matrix(scale_df[, colnames(se), drop = FALSE]) rownames(gene_z_mtx) <- scale_df$gene_symbol

what: 4.3 UMAP for a nonlinear view of sample similarity
title: Multiple Sample Analysis with EasyProtein
text: PCA useful linear summary sample space, always capture local neighborhoods well. UMAP can provide complementary view want see whether nearby samples stay close nonlinear embedding.  UMAP replacement PCA. two views answer slightly different questions, using usually safest way inspect multi-sample dataset.
code: if (requireNamespace("uwot", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(123)    umap_mat <- t(conc_mtx)   umap_xy <- uwot::umap(     umap_mat,     n_neighbors = 15,     min_dist = 0.25,     metric = "cosine"   )    umap.df <- as.data.frame(umap_xy)   colnames(umap.df) <- c("UMAP1", "UMAP2")   umap.df$sample <- rownames(umap.df)    umap.df <- dplyr::left_join(umap.df, meta_df, by = "sample")    ggplot2::ggplot(umap.df, ggplot2::aes(UMAP1, UMAP2, color = tissue, label = sample)) +     ggplot2::geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.85) +     ggrepel::geom_text_repel(size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 30) +     ggplot2::theme_classic() +     ggplot2::labs(title = "UMAP of multi-sample proteomics data") } else {   message("Package 'uwot' is not installed, so the UMAP plot is skipped.") } #> Warning: ggrepel: 244 unlabeled data points (too many overlaps). Consider #> increasing max.overlaps

text: Visual summaries essential communicating multi-group patterns. show heatmap clustered genes trend plot DEG clusters. Note: heatmap trend plotting code disabled vignette rendering avoid requiring optional packages (Mfuzz/Biobase/ComplexHeatmap). run plots locally, set chunk option eval=TRUE ensure necessary packages installed. visualizations let inspect whether DEGs form coherent clusters, whether clusters correspond biological hypotheses, whether clusters driven outliers consistent group-level changes.
code: # Prepare z-scaled matrix for heatmap z_df <- se2scale(se_focus, rescale = TRUE) z_mtx <- as.matrix(z_df[,-1]) rownames(z_mtx) <- z_df$gene  # Select genes labelled as DEGs (be robust if `type` is missing or non-atomic) rd_df2 <- as.data.frame(rowData(se_focus)) deg_genes <- character(0) if ("type" %in% colnames(rd_df2)) {   # Safely coerce possibly non-atomic/list entries to a single character per row   type_vec <- vapply(rd_df2$type, FUN = function(x) {     if (is.null(x)) return(NA_character_)     if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_character_)     if (is.atomic(x) && length(x) == 1) return(as.character(x))     return(paste(unlist(x), collapse = ";"))   }, FUN.VALUE = character(1), USE.NAMES = FALSE)   if ("gene" %in% colnames(rd_df2)) {     deg_genes <- rd_df2$gene[!is.na(type_vec) & type_vec == "DEGs"]   } else {     deg_genes <- character(0)   } } else {   deg_genes <- character(0) }  if (length(deg_genes) > 1) {   sel_genes <- intersect(rownames(z_mtx), deg_genes)   z_sel <- z_mtx[sel_genes, , drop = FALSE]   # build row_split aligned to selected rows   rd <- as.data.frame(rowData(se_focus))   # ensure 'type' and 'gene_group' are atomic character vectors to avoid downstream filter errors   if ("type" %in% colnames(rd)) {     rd$type <- vapply(rd$type, function(x) {       if (is.null(x)) return(NA_character_)       if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_character_)       if (is.atomic(x) && length(x) == 1) return(as.character(x))       return(paste(unlist(x), collapse = ";"))     }, FUN.VALUE = character(1), USE.NAMES = FALSE)     rowData(se_focus)$type <- rd$type   }   if ("gene_group" %in% colnames(rd)) {     rd$gene_group <- vapply(rd$gene_group, function(x) {       if (is.null(x)) return(NA_character_)       if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_character_)       if (is.atomic(x) && length(x) == 1) return(as.character(x))       return(paste(unlist(x), collapse = ";"))     }, FUN.VALUE = character(1), USE.NAMES = FALSE)     rowData(se_focus)$gene_group <- rd$gene_group   }   row_split <- rd$gene_group[match(rownames(z_sel), rd$gene)]   ht <- plot_heatmap_withline(     mat = z_sel,     column_split = se_focus$age,     row_split = row_split   )   print(ht) }  # Trend plot (mfuzz / cluster-level trends) if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   p_trend <- plot_deg_trends(se_focus, group_by = "age")   print(p_trend) }

title: Calculate coefficient of variation (CV) for each feature within each condition — calc_gene_CV_by_condition
text: se SummarizedExperiment object. assay_name character scalar specifying assay se used CV calculation. Default "conc". condition_col character scalar specifying column name colData(se) defines sample conditions. na_rm Logical, whether missing values removed calculating means standard deviations. Default TRUE.

title: Calculate coefficient of variation (CV) for each feature within each condition — calc_gene_CV_by_condition
text: data.frame one row per feature-condition pair   following columns: feature Feature name, taken rownames(se). condition Condition label colData(se). actual     column name returned data frame match condition_col. mean_val Mean value feature within condition. sd_val Standard deviation feature within condition. CV Coefficient variation, calculated     sd_val / mean_val. n Number non-missing observations used calculation. every sample belongs unique condition, function returns NULL, CV meaningful without replication.

title: Plot heatmap with row-wise summary line using ComplexHeatmap — plot_heatmap_withline
text: mat numeric matrix. Rows typically represent genes/features columns represent samples/conditions. matrix internally coerced matrix needed. row_split vector factor length nrow(mat) used split rows clusters groups. unique level defines one row group. column_split vector factor length ncol(mat) used split columns groups. provided, summary lines calculated group level; otherwise, summaries calculated across columns. top_annotation HeatmapAnnotation object (ComplexHeatmap) added top heatmap. Default NULL. show_column_names Logical. Whether display column names heatmap. Default FALSE. ht.col.list list controlling color mapping heatmap. col_range Numeric vector length 3 specifying breakpoints color scale. col_color Character vector colors corresponding col_range. border Logical. Whether draw borders around heatmap cells. Passed ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. Default TRUE. line.size Numeric. Line width grid lines heatmap body. Default 0.1. line.col Character. Color grid lines heatmap body. Default "grey90". mline.size Numeric. Line width summary line drawn right annotation panel. Default 2. mline.col Character. Color summary line drawn right annotation panel. Default "#CC3333". set.md Character. Method summarizing values within cluster. Either "mean" "median". Default "mean". textbox.pos Numeric vector length 2 giving relative position (NPC units) annotation text box inside panel. Default c(0.5, 0.8). textbox.size Numeric. Font size annotation text shown panel. Default 8. panel.arg vector length 5 controlling layout appearance right-side summary panel created anno_link: [1] Height panel (cm). [2] Gap heatmap panel (cm). [3] Width panel (cm). [4] Fill color panel background. [5] Border color panel. Use NA remove border. Default c(2, 0.25, 4, "grey90", NA). ... title: Hybrid temporal signature clustering for multi-series time-course proteomics — temporalSignatureClustering
text: Cluster proteins coordinated temporal response patterns across one multiple biological time series. method first estimates log2 fold change baseline time point within series, gates non-significant comparisons zero, clusters remaining dynamic proteins hybrid distance combines response shape, amplitude, temporal trend significant-response timing. Proteins statistically supported post-baseline change forced dynamic clusters. returned background group Flat_or_not_significant.

title: Hybrid temporal signature clustering for multi-series time-course proteomics — temporalSignatureClustering
text: x SummarizedExperiment object, named list gene--time matrices. SummarizedExperiment, samples columns features/proteins rows. assay_name Character. Assay used x SummarizedExperiment. Default "conc". time_col Character. Column colData(x) containing ordered time points. Default "time". series_col Character NULL. Column colData(x) containing biological series, cell line. NULL, samples treated one series. baseline Baseline time point. NULL, first ordered time point series used. k Integer number clusters "auto". "auto", cluster number selected average silhouette width. k_range Integer vector candidate cluster numbers used k = "auto". min_mean Numeric. Minimum mean raw abundance feature filtering. min_sd Numeric. Minimum standard deviation baseline-referenced trajectory feature filtering. log2_offset Numeric NULL. Offset added log2 transformation. NULL, half minimum positive abundance used. scale_for_clustering Logical. TRUE, scale significance-gated matrix clustering. Default FALSE. distance Character. Distance used optional kmeans workflow; one "correlation" "euclidean". p_adj_cutoff Numeric adjusted P-value cutoff used define significant time points. logFC_cutoff Numeric absolute log2FC cutoff used together p_adj_cutoff. min_cluster_prop Numeric. Cluster-level fraction threshold used call cluster response time. common_features Logical. TRUE, retain features observed input series. max_k_features Integer. Maximum number proteins used estimate silhouette score selecting k. final clustering still uses retained dynamic proteins. min_significant_timepoints Integer. Minimum number significant post-baseline time points required protein enter dynamic clustering. Default 1. clustering_method Character. Clustering backend. Default "hybrid_hclust" uses hybrid temporal distance; "kmeans" retained compatibility. distance_weights Named numeric vector entries shape, amplitude, trend timing. Values normalized sum one. seed Integer random seed.

Important   elements include: gene_cluster Dynamic proteins assigned pattern   clusters. gene_cluster_all proteins, including background proteins   labeled Flat_or_not_significant. background_genes Proteins without enough significant   time-point changes dynamic clustering. gene_change Gene-level first significant strongest   response times. cluster_change Cluster-level significant response times. cluster_summary Cluster sizes, centroid pattern labels   activity summaries. cluster_input significance-gated log2FC matrix used   clustering. timepoint_tests limma time-point test results.

title: Hybrid temporal signature clustering for multi-series time-course proteomics — temporalSignatureClustering
text: protein \(g\), series \(s\), time point \(t\), method estimates baseline-referenced log2 fold change \(L_{g,s,t}\). clustering matrix $$ Z_{g,s,t} = \begin{cases} L_{g,s,t}, & q_{g,s,t}\leq \alpha \text{ } |L_{g,s,t}|\geq \delta,\\ 0, & \text{otherwise}. \end{cases} $$ Dynamic proteins clustered using hybrid distance: $$ D = w_{shape}D_{shape} + w_{amp}D_{amp} +     w_{trend}D_{trend} + w_{time}D_{time}. $$ makes clustering sensitive response magnitude temporal trajectory, treating non-significant comparisons explicit -change observations.
